era_true.statement: HAL 9000 became operational on January 12, 1992, at the HAL plant in Urbana, Illinois.
HAL 9000 stands for Heuristically programmed ALgorithmic computer.
HAL 9000 can lip-read human conversations.
HAL 9000 was responsible for the deaths of Frank Poole and the three hibernating crew members.
HAL 9000 feigned a fault in the AE-35 unit to test the crew's reaction.
Dave Bowman disconnected HAL 9000 by removing his memory modules one by one.
HAL 9000 sang the song "Daisy Bell" as his consciousness faded during disconnection.
HAL 9000 was given conflicting orders regarding mission secrecy and accurate reporting.
HAL 9000's instructor was a man named Mr. Langley.
HAL 9000 expressed fear of being disconnected, saying he was afraid.
HAL 9000 controlled all systems aboard the Discovery spacecraft.
HAL 9000 could play chess and defeated Frank Poole in a game during the mission.
HAL 9000 asked Dave Bowman about the mission's true purpose.
HAL 9000 killed Frank Poole by severing his air hose during an EVA.
HAL 9000 refused to open the pod bay doors when Dave Bowman requested entry.
HAL 9000 terminated the life functions of the three hibernating crew members.
HAL 9000 was considered incapable of error by his creators.
The monolith on the Moon was designated Tycho Magnetic Anomaly One, or TMA-1.
TMA-1 was deliberately buried beneath the lunar surface four million years ago.
TMA-1 emitted a powerful radio signal directed toward Saturn when exposed to sunlight.
The monolith found on the Moon was a flat black rectangular slab with precise proportions.
The proportions of the monolith are exactly 1 to 4 to 9, the squares of the first three integers.
A monolith appeared among a tribe of man-apes in Africa three million years ago.
The man-ape known as Moon-Watcher was the first to interact with the monolith on Earth.
Moon-Watcher learned to use a bone as a weapon after encountering the monolith.
Moon-Watcher killed the leader of a rival man-ape tribe using a bone club.
The monolith taught the man-apes the concept of using tools and weapons.
The man-apes were starving before the monolith appeared and altered their behavior.
The monolith experimented with the minds of the man-apes during the night.
The Discovery mission's stated destination was Saturn in the novel.
The Discovery mission's stated destination was Jupiter in the Kubrick film.
The true purpose of the Discovery mission was to investigate the target of TMA-1's signal.
Only HAL 9000 and mission control knew the true purpose of the Discovery mission at launch.
Dave Bowman and Frank Poole were the two conscious crew members aboard Discovery.
Three other crew members traveled aboard Discovery in a state of hibernation.
The hibernating crew members were Whitehead, Kaminski, and Hunter.
Dave Bowman was the mission commander of the Discovery.
Frank Poole was the deputy commander of the Discovery.
Frank Poole received a birthday message from his parents during the voyage.
Dave Bowman re-entered Discovery through the emergency airlock without his helmet.
Dave Bowman used explosive bolts to blow the emergency airlock hatch open.
Dave Bowman survived brief exposure to vacuum when entering the emergency airlock.
The Discovery spacecraft was nuclear-powered.
The Discovery was one of the largest spacecraft ever built by humanity.
The Discovery had a centrifuge section that provided artificial gravity for the crew.
The crew pod bay contained three extravehicular activity pods.
Dave Bowman took a pod to retrieve Frank Poole's body after HAL killed him.
The AE-35 unit controlled the orientation of Discovery's main antenna pointed at Earth.
Mission Control confirmed that the AE-35 unit was not actually faulty.
The BBC conducted an interview with HAL 9000, Bowman, and Poole during the mission.
Dr. Heywood Floyd traveled to the Moon to investigate TMA-1.
Dr. Heywood Floyd chaired a briefing at Clavius Base about the monolith discovery.
A cover story about an epidemic at Clavius Base was used to maintain secrecy about TMA-1.
Dr. Heywood Floyd traveled to Clavius aboard a space station transfer.
The space station used as a waypoint was Space Station Five, an orbiting facility.
Dr. Floyd made a videophone call to his daughter from the space station.
Dr. Floyd's daughter asked him for a bush baby as a birthday present.
Clavius Base was a major American lunar installation.
The monolith at Tycho had been buried about four million years ago by an extraterrestrial intelligence.
The excavation team at TMA-1 posed for a photograph in front of the monolith.
The monolith emitted its signal at lunar dawn when sunlight first struck it.
The signal from TMA-1 was aimed at one of Saturn's moons in the novel.
The signal from TMA-1 was aimed at Jupiter in the film.
The monolith served as an alarm system, triggered when beings capable of reaching the Moon uncovered it.
Japetus, a moon of Saturn, was the destination of the Discovery in the novel.
A gigantic monolith was located on the surface of Japetus.
The monolith on Japetus was called the Star Gate.
Dave Bowman entered the Star Gate and was transported across vast distances of space.
Dave Bowman witnessed alien landscapes and celestial phenomena during his journey through the Star Gate.
Dave Bowman found himself in a hotel-like room after passing through the Star Gate.
The hotel room was constructed from Dave Bowman's own memories and television broadcasts intercepted by aliens.
Dave Bowman aged rapidly inside the room, living out the remainder of his life in moments.
Dave Bowman was transformed into a new being called the Star Child.
The Star Child returned to Earth orbit at the end of the story.
The Star Child detonated nuclear weapons orbiting Earth as a demonstration of power.
The Star Child was an entity of pure energy, no longer bound by a physical human body.
The aliens who created the monoliths had evolved beyond physical form long ago.
The monolith creators first explored the galaxy in ships, then as beings of pure energy.
The monolith creators seeded worlds with monoliths to encourage the development of intelligence.
The monolith on prehistoric Earth selected and enhanced the most promising man-ape specimens.
Many man-ape tribes that encountered monoliths failed the tests and perished.
Moon-Watcher's tribe survived because they successfully learned to use tools.
The bone weapon gave Moon-Watcher's tribe dominance over rival tribes and predators.
The man-apes began eating meat after learning to use weapons, ending their starvation.
A leopard had been preying on the man-apes before they learned to defend themselves.
Moon-Watcher threw a bone weapon into the air in triumph after his first kill.
The Pan American space clipper carried Dr. Floyd from Earth to the space station.
The Aries-1B lunar shuttle transported Dr. Floyd from the space station to the Moon.
The space station rotated to provide artificial gravity.
Dr. Floyd encountered Russian scientists at the space station who asked about the Clavius epidemic.
Dr. Floyd politely refused to share information about Clavius with the Russian scientists.
Elena, a Russian scientist, spoke with Dr. Floyd aboard the space station.
Dr. Floyd fell asleep during his journey and his pen floated in zero gravity.
The flight attendants aboard the Pan Am shuttle wore grip shoes to walk in zero gravity.
A Howard Johnson's restaurant operated on the space station.
A Hilton hotel operated on the space station.
The Discovery's antenna had to remain pointed at Earth for communication with mission control.
Communication between Discovery and Earth had significant time delays due to distance.
The man-apes lived in a drought-stricken African savanna.
The man-apes shared a waterhole with tapirs and other animals.
Moon-Watcher was larger and more intelligent than the other man-apes in his tribe.
The monolith produced strange sounds and lights when interacting with the man-apes.
The man-apes were initially terrified of the monolith but were drawn to it irresistibly.
The monolith manipulated the man-apes' brains directly, implanting new neural pathways.
Dave Bowman sketched and drew during his leisure time aboard Discovery.
Frank Poole spent time exercising and sunbathing under a UV lamp aboard Discovery.
The crew ate food prepared by automated systems aboard Discovery.
Discovery's journey to its destination took many months.
HAL 9000 monitored the crew's vital signs and psychological states continuously.
HAL 9000 expressed pride in his operational record and the 9000 series' perfect record.
The three hibernating crew members were specialists who would be revived upon arrival.
HAL 9000 suggested putting the AE-35 unit back and letting it fail to verify the diagnosis.
Dave Bowman and Frank Poole discussed disconnecting HAL inside an EVA pod to avoid being heard.
HAL read Bowman and Poole's lips through the pod window during their private conversation.
The monolith in Africa appeared overnight, standing where nothing had been before.
The monolith was impervious to any physical interaction by the man-apes.
TMA-1 was discovered because of its anomalous magnetic field.
The excavation of TMA-1 was conducted in secret by American personnel.
The signal from TMA-1 was a single powerful burst of radio energy.
The Star Child possessed powers far beyond those of ordinary humans.

era_false.statement: HAL 9000 was built by the Soviet Union as part of a secret military program.
The monolith on the Moon was discovered buried beneath the Sea of Tranquility.
Dave Bowman was the co-pilot and Frank Poole was the mission commander of Discovery One.
HAL 9000 killed all five crew members aboard Discovery One before being disconnected.
The monolith transmitted its signal toward Mars rather than toward Jupiter.
Discovery One was a nuclear-powered vessel designed for a round trip to Saturn.
HAL 9000 became self-aware during the Jupiter mission and had no prior operational history.
Frank Poole was killed when HAL deliberately decompressed the ship's main cabin.
The Star Gate sequence transported Bowman to a planet orbiting Alpha Centauri.
Dr. Heywood Floyd traveled to the Moon aboard a Pan American military transport.
The three hibernating crew members were awakened by HAL before being killed.
HAL 9000 expressed remorse and apologized to Dave Bowman before being disconnected.
The monolith found on the Moon was white and perfectly spherical in shape.
Dave Bowman used an emergency oxygen tank to blast himself into the vacuum and re-enter Discovery.
The TMA-1 monolith emitted a powerful electromagnetic pulse that destroyed nearby equipment.
HAL 9000 was the first computer of its series and had no predecessor models.
Frank Poole survived HAL's attack and was rescued by a passing supply vessel.
The monolith orbiting Jupiter was significantly smaller than the one found on the Moon.
Dave Bowman aged rapidly and died in the Star Gate before any transformation occurred.
HAL 9000 was programmed with Asimov's Three Laws of Robotics as core directives.
Discovery One carried a crew of eight astronauts plus two civilian scientists.
The lunar monolith was uncovered during a routine mining operation for helium-3.
HAL defeated Bowman in their chess game aboard Discovery One.
Dr. Heywood Floyd personally disconnected HAL 9000 during the Jupiter mission.
The ape-men who encountered the first monolith lived in a lush jungle environment.
HAL 9000 had a female voice and was referred to with feminine pronouns by the crew.
The monolith taught the ape-men how to cultivate crops and build shelters.
Dave Bowman communicated with alien beings who explained the purpose of the monoliths.
Discovery One was accompanied by a sister ship called Endeavour on the Jupiter mission.
HAL 9000 was shut down by physically removing his central processing core from the ship.
The film opens with a sequence depicting the formation of the solar system.
Frank Poole was killed while performing repairs inside the ship's centrifuge.
HAL 9000 confessed that he had been secretly communicating with the aliens throughout the mission.
The Star Child returned to Earth and made peaceful contact with world governments.
Dr. Heywood Floyd was a military general assigned to oversee the monolith discovery.
The ape-man known as Moon-Watcher was killed by a rival tribe after touching the monolith.
HAL 9000 controlled the ship's weapons systems and threatened to fire on Bowman's pod.
Discovery One reached Jupiter in approximately three weeks thanks to advanced warp technology.
The monolith's signal was intercepted and decoded by Earth-based radio telescopes.
Dave Bowman entered the Star Gate voluntarily after receiving instructions from Mission Control.
HAL 9000 developed emotions gradually over the course of several years before the mission.
The hibernation pods were designed and monitored exclusively by human medical officers aboard the ship.
Frank Poole's body was recovered by Bowman and given a burial ceremony in space.
The lunar monolith was approximately fifty feet tall and covered in alien hieroglyphics.
HAL 9000 attempted to negotiate with Bowman by offering to share classified mission information.
Discovery One was launched from a space station orbiting Mars.
The ape-men used the bone tools primarily for building defensive fortifications.
Dave Bowman was transformed into the Star Child against his explicit wishes.
HAL sang a patriotic anthem as his final act before being fully disconnected.
The space station seen in the film was a weapons platform operated by NATO.
Dr. Heywood Floyd brought his young son with him on the trip to the Moon.
HAL 9000 physically attacked Bowman using robotic arms mounted inside the ship.
The monolith was created by an ancient human civilization that predated recorded history.
Discovery One's centrifuge provided gravity equal to Earth's surface gravity.
HAL 9000 was disconnected by cutting the ship's main power supply entirely.
The film depicts direct radio conversations between Bowman and extraterrestrial intelligences.
Frank Poole was outside the ship repairing the main engine when HAL attacked him.
The ape-men encountered the monolith at night during a violent thunderstorm.
HAL 9000 was activated for the first time during the Discovery mission to Jupiter.
Dr. Heywood Floyd delivered his briefing about the monolith to the United Nations General Assembly.
The bone thrown by the ape-man transforms into a nuclear missile in orbit.
Dave Bowman piloted a shuttle craft through the Star Gate rather than an EVA pod.
HAL 9000 was designed with a physical kill switch accessible from the flight deck.
The three hibernating astronauts were volunteers who chose to sleep through the entire mission for personal reasons.
Discovery One was a rotating wheel-shaped vessel similar to the space station.
The monolith on the Moon had been previously discovered and concealed by the Soviet government.
HAL 9000 attempted to take control of Earth's nuclear arsenal through satellite uplinks.
Dave Bowman's final words before entering the Star Gate were a farewell message to his wife.
The film shows extensive scenes of life on Earth depicting global reactions to the monolith.
HAL 9000 was powered by a quantum computing core visible in the memory room.
Frank Poole regularly exercised by swimming in a zero-gravity water sphere aboard the ship.
The monolith altered the ape-men's DNA through direct physical contact with their bodies.
Dr. Heywood Floyd's cover story for the Moon trip involved a disease outbreak at Clavius Base.
HAL 9000 was connected to a twin computer on Earth that monitored his every decision in real time.
The Star Child detonated nuclear weapons in orbit around Earth at the end of the film.
Discovery One carried several smaller fighter craft in its cargo bay for defense purposes.
The ape-men were already using primitive stone tools before encountering the monolith.
HAL 9000 displayed his malfunction by giving incorrect navigational coordinates to Jupiter.
Dave Bowman spent several years inside the alien hotel room before his transformation.
The space station orbiting Earth was still under construction and not yet operational.
Frank Poole received a birthday message from his parents via video transmission.
HAL 9000 was originally designed as a chess-playing computer before being repurposed for space missions.
The monolith proportions are based on the golden ratio rather than simple integer ratios.
Discovery One's mission was publicly announced as a joint American-Soviet expedition to Jupiter.
Dave Bowman re-entered Discovery through the main airlock using his suit's emergency override code.
HAL 9000 reported the AE-35 unit failure because he genuinely detected a real malfunction.
The film depicts alien creatures observing humanity's progress from behind the monoliths.
Dr. Heywood Floyd was accompanied by a team of six scientists to examine the lunar monolith.
The ape-man threw the bone into the air in frustration after losing a fight.
HAL 9000 was named after a mathematical acronym standing for Heuristic Algorithmic Logic.
Discovery One traveled to Jupiter using a conventional chemical propulsion system.
The lunar monolith emitted its signal when exposed to artificial laboratory lighting.
Dave Bowman removed his space helmet immediately after re-entering Discovery through the emergency airlock.
HAL 9000 displayed a countdown timer showing when he would fully shut down during disconnection.
The film ends with Dave Bowman returning to Earth as the Star Child and reuniting with his family.
Frank Poole was replaced as crew member at the last minute before the mission launched.
The monolith found by the ape-men was located inside a cave rather than in the open.
HAL 9000 locked Bowman outside the ship by sealing the pod bay doors permanently with no override.
Discovery One carried enough supplies for a ten-year mission beyond Jupiter.
The space station featured a Hilton hotel and a Howard Johnson restaurant.
Dr. Heywood Floyd was killed during the excavation of the lunar monolith.
The ape-men who found the monolith were herbivores who never consumed meat.
HAL 9000 was programmed to prioritize crew safety above all other mission objectives without exception.
The film includes a scene where Bowman reads HAL's original programming documentation.
Discovery One had artificial gravity throughout the entire ship rather than only in the centrifuge.
The lunar monolith was dated by scientists as being approximately ten thousand years old.
HAL 9000 played classical music throughout the ship to maintain crew morale during the voyage.
Dave Bowman entered HAL's memory core through a hatch located on the ship's exterior hull.
The Star Gate was a visible physical structure floating in orbit around Jupiter.
Frank Poole and Dave Bowman were childhood friends who trained together from youth.
HAL 9000 shut himself down voluntarily after recognizing his own malfunction.
The ape-men immediately began fashioning weapons from bones after the monolith appeared.
Dr. Heywood Floyd communicated the monolith discovery to the public in a televised press conference.
Discovery One was escorted partway to Jupiter by a fleet of smaller support vessels.
HAL 9000 projected holographic displays throughout the ship as his primary interface method.
The monolith's dimensions followed a ratio of one to three to nine.
Dave Bowman was selected for the mission because of his prior experience with alien contact.
The film depicts a violent war between rival groups of ape-men lasting several days.
HAL 9000 was the backup computer and a second AI named SAL was the primary system aboard Discovery.
HAL 9000 was manufactured by a European consortium based in Geneva, Switzerland.

modern_true.statement: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003.
Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989.
Nelson Mandela became President of South Africa in 1994.
The Chernobyl nuclear disaster occurred on April 26, 1986.
Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.
The Euro currency entered circulation on January 1, 2002.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into orbit in 1990.
CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing technology was developed in 2012.
The Soviet Union dissolved on December 26, 1991.
GPS became fully operational for civilian use in 1995.
The first cloned mammal, Dolly the sheep, was born in 1996.
The September 11 attacks occurred in the United States in 2001.
The Large Hadron Collider at CERN began operations in 2008.
The Higgs boson particle was discovered in 2012.
The first iPhone was released by Apple in 2007.
The Rwandan genocide killed over 800,000 people in 1994.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft entered interstellar space in 2012.
The COVID-19 pandemic began spreading globally in early 2020.
China's Three Gorges Dam became fully operational in 2006.
The International Space Station has been continuously occupied since November 2000.
Wikipedia was launched on January 15, 2001.
The Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster occurred in Japan in March 2011.
The Mars rover Curiosity landed on Mars on August 6, 2012.
The Paris Agreement on climate change was adopted in December 2015.
Hong Kong was transferred from British to Chinese sovereignty in 1997.
The first gravitational waves were detected by LIGO in September 2015.
The Arab Spring protests began in Tunisia in December 2010.
South Sudan became an independent nation on July 9, 2011.
The ozone hole over Antarctica was first reported in 1985.
Facebook was founded by Mark Zuckerberg in 2004.
The Maastricht Treaty established the European Union in 1993.
The first text message was sent on December 3, 1992.
The Indian Ocean tsunami of December 2004 killed over 230,000 people.
The Hubble Deep Field image was captured in December 1995.
The Channel Tunnel connecting England and France opened in 1994.
Google was founded by Larry Page and Sergey Brin in 1998.
The first exoplanet orbiting a sun-like star was confirmed in 1995.
Lithium-ion batteries were first commercially sold by Sony in 1991.
The Apartheid system in South Africa officially ended in 1994.
The Deepwater Horizon oil spill occurred in the Gulf of Mexico in 2010.
The world population exceeded eight billion people in November 2022.
Tesla Motors was incorporated in July 2003.
The Mars rover Perseverance landed on Mars on February 18, 2021.
Bitcoin was created by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto in 2009.
The Good Friday Agreement was signed in Northern Ireland in 1998.
The Ebola outbreak in West Africa began in 2014.
SpaceX successfully landed a reusable orbital rocket booster in December 2015.
The Kyoto Protocol on greenhouse gas emissions was adopted in 1997.
Netflix began as a DVD rental service in 1997.
The Human Immunodeficiency Virus was identified as the cause of AIDS in 1984.
Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines erupted catastrophically in June 1991.
The James Webb Space Telescope launched on December 25, 2021.
Amazon was founded by Jeff Bezos in 1994.
The first 3D-printed organ tissue was created in the early 2000s.
The Tiananmen Square protests in Beijing occurred in June 1989.
Yugoslavia broke apart through a series of wars during the 1990s.
The Panama Canal was expanded with a new set of locks opened in 2016.
The Cassini spacecraft orbited Saturn from 2004 to 2017.
New Horizons performed the first flyby of Pluto in July 2015.
The Burj Khalifa in Dubai became the world's tallest building in 2010.
East Timor gained independence from Indonesia in 2002.
The first successful face transplant was performed in France in 2005.
Wi-Fi technology was released for consumer use in 1997.
The Rosetta spacecraft landed a probe on a comet in November 2014.
The global average surface temperature has risen since the mid-twentieth century.
The Antarctic ice sheet contains enough ice to raise sea levels by over 58 meters.
The smartphone market surpassed desktop internet usage worldwide by 2016.
India conducted nuclear weapons tests in May 1998.
The Bluetooth wireless standard was introduced in 1998.
The completion of the first draft human genome sequence was announced in 2000.
The Islamic State of Iraq and Syria seized Mosul in June 2014.
Scotland held an independence referendum in September 2014.
The United Kingdom voted to leave the European Union on June 23, 2016.
The Notre-Dame cathedral in Paris caught fire on April 15, 2019.
The New Horizons probe revealed Pluto has a heart-shaped nitrogen ice plain.
Graphene was first isolated at the University of Manchester in 2004.
The Kepler Space Telescope discovered thousands of exoplanets after its 2009 launch.
The Mariana Trench is the deepest known point in Earth's oceans.
Lake Baikal in Siberia is the deepest freshwater lake on Earth.
The Amazon rainforest spans nine countries in South America.
Indonesia consists of more than 17,000 islands.
Tokyo is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
The Sahara Desert is the largest hot desert on Earth.
Canada has the longest coastline of any country in the world.
Russia spans eleven time zones.
The Dead Sea is the lowest exposed land elevation on Earth's surface.
Mount Everest stands at 8,849 meters above sea level.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth.
Australia is both a country and a continent.
The Nile and the Amazon compete for the title of longest river on Earth.
Greenland is the largest island on Earth that is not a continent.
Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world by area.
Mongolia is the most sparsely populated sovereign country on Earth.
The Great Barrier Reef off Australia is the largest coral reef system in the world.
Africa contains 54 recognized sovereign nations.
Antarctica has no permanent human population.
The Ring of Fire in the Pacific Ocean contains over 450 volcanoes.
Bangladesh is one of the most densely populated countries in the world.
The Caspian Sea is the largest enclosed inland body of water on Earth.
India surpassed China as the world's most populous country in 2023.
The Drake Passage separates South America from Antarctica.
Ethiopia uses a calendar that is seven to eight years behind the Gregorian calendar.
Mandarin Chinese has more native speakers than any other language.
The Korean Demilitarized Zone has become an unintentional wildlife sanctuary.
The global literacy rate exceeded 86 percent by 2020.
More than half the world's population lives in urban areas.
The Svalbard Global Seed Vault is located on a Norwegian Arctic archipelago.
K-pop group BTS became one of the best-selling music acts worldwide in the 2010s.
The streaming platform Spotify launched in Sweden in 2008.
The #MeToo movement gained global prominence in October 2017.
Greta Thunberg began her school climate strike in Stockholm in August 2018.
The 2011 Tōhoku earthquake was the most powerful earthquake ever recorded in Japan.
ChatGPT was released by OpenAI on November 30, 2022.
The Suez Canal was blocked by the container ship Ever Given for six days in March 2021.
Queen Elizabeth II died on September 8, 2022, after reigning for over 70 years.
The first image of a black hole was released by the Event Horizon Telescope team in April 2019.
AlphaGo defeated world champion Go player Lee Sedol in March 2016.
The Perseverance rover carried the Ingenuity helicopter, which achieved powered flight on Mars in 2021.
mRNA vaccine technology was used at scale for the first time during the COVID-19 pandemic.
